Identity.History: Cyrus the Great ruled from about 559–530 BC and founded the Achaemenid Persian Empire. Beginning as king of Anshan, he overthrew the Median Empire, conquered Lydia, and captured Babylon in 539 BC, creating the largest empire yet seen. Cyrus was remembered for political skill, military brilliance, and relatively tolerant rule toward conquered peoples, including respect for local customs and religions. His policies helped unify diverse lands from Iran to Mesopotamia and Anatolia. He died campaigning on the empire’s northeastern frontier.
